name: Noah Fant
headline: Saints move tight end faces uphill volume battle
body:
Fant lands in New Orleans after a diminished 2025 in Cincinnati, where his role shrank to a 37.9 percent snap share and 215 routes, turning 41 targets into 34 catches for just 288 yards and 3 touchdowns, 40.8 fantasy points and a TE41 finish. That short-area usage, a 3.34 air yards per target and 8.47 yards per catch, plus three fumbles and a negative 0.287 EPA per play, marked his least productive receiving season since becoming a regular. It was a steep drop from his 2024 Seattle line of 500 yards on 64 targets.

The move to New Orleans offers a chance at more route volume, but he still profiles as a complementary, TD-dependent streamer rather than a featured option, and the Saints` shaky quarterback situation caps the ceiling. His best season here looks like the high-40s fantasy points his recent profile suggests. For 2026 he is a low-end TE2 and matchup-based streaming candidate only, not a set-and-forget weekly starter. Leave him on the wire in 10- and 12-team leagues.
